title: Department of Software Engineering (English) - About The Internship
content:
Department of Software Engineering (English) - About The Internship

Students in the Software Engineering department are required to complete a compulsory summer internship totaling

. This is split into two periods: 20 working days during the summer of their second year and another 20 working days during the summer of their third year.

Areas for internships

include software companies, the defense industry, or electronics and communication companies.

Before Starting the Internship:

The

"Internship Application Form"

must be filled out, signed by the Head of the Software Engineering Department and the Dean of the Faculty of Engineering and Architecture, and then submitted to the internship workplace.

The

"Compulsory Internship Form"

must be filled out in duplicate. Both copies need to be signed and stamped by the supervisor at the internship workplace, have a passport-sized photo attached, and be submitted to the department along with two copies of your ID at least

the internship begins.

When arranging the start and end dates to ensure 20 working days, it's crucial to

exclude official holidays, public holidays, and weekends

.

After Completing the Internship:

The

internship report

is expected to be written in

English

, using technical language. The work documented in the report should be supported with code snippets, images, or screenshots.

No day should be left blank

in the report.

Once completed,

every page of the internship report

must be signed and stamped by the supervisor at the internship workplace.

The

"Performance Evaluation Form"

(Sicil Belgesi) will be filled out by the supervisor at the internship workplace, placed in a sealed envelope, and signed across the seal. This sealed envelope, along with the internship report, must be submitted to the department after the internship is completed.

This content was updated on 29/07/2025.
